summary: Retested ONLY the checkout-redirect fix as requested. End-to-end pickup-order flow with COD works correctly: after clicking Place Order the browser navigates to /order/<id> (NOT back to /bag). Order tracking page renders the order number, items, summary and a status timeline with 'Waiting for confirmation' marked as the current step. Cleanup PATCH /api/admin/orders/{id}/status cancelled the test order successfully (HTTP 200, status=cancelled).
critical_code_review_comments: Checkout.jsx fix is implemented correctly: `placing` flag prevents the empty-bag redirect effect (lines 72-75) from firing during the navigate→clear race; navigate uses {replace:true} and clear() is queued via setTimeout(...,0) after navigation commits.

Minor: on payment-required (Tap) branch, `clear()` runs before `window.location.href = data.payment_url` — if the user is sent back to /bag before the redirect occurs (slow networks) the empty-bag effect could fire. Not in scope for this iteration but worth noting.
seed_data_creation: Created and immediately cancelled one test order id=5247eb46-b50e-4ec6-9292-6944185486ef (#LMZ-20260512-3123). No residual data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Checkout redirect bug is fully resolved. Order placement → /order/<id> navigation verified. Order tracking UI displays order number, items, totals and status timeline correctly. Backend admin cancel endpoint works. Future testers can reuse the same E2E flow: add to bag → /bag → /checkout → Pickup → fill name/phone → COD → Place Order.
rca of the issue: Previously after `clear()` ran synchronously post-POST, the items array became empty BEFORE React Router committed the navigate('/order/<id>'). The useEffect at lines 72-75 watching `items.length===0` then re-redirected the user to /bag. Fix: (1) added `placing` state; (2) effect early-returns when placing=true; (3) navigate with {replace:true} fires before clear; (4) clear is deferred via setTimeout(...,0) so the route change commits first. Verified in this iteration: navigation lands on /order/<id> and stays there.
